summary: Core CLI flows were usable: clone/run/tell/view/stop/remove worked for @clier/hello-claude, local edits were detected and pushed with ref cascades, server-side drift surfaced through status/fetch/pull, and JSON output stayed parseable. The main trust breaks were discoverability and state transparency: `run note` only exists in agent mode, read-after-write consistency lagged by about two seconds on create/fork/delete, `pull` gives a removal-oriented dirty-workspace hint, referenced skills can be deleted while still pinned by teams, org commands require an undocumented @ prefix after creation, and version-pinned `fork` syntax is misparsed.

findings.title: Version and auth status are reachable from user mode
findings.rationale: `clier --version` and `clier auth status` both succeeded from user mode at the start of the run, so the rest of the QA flow could execute against a live authenticated CLI.

findings.title: `run note` is hidden behind agent mode and undiscoverable from user help
findings.rationale: The work-log protocol told me to use `clier run note`, but user-mode help neither lists it nor accepts it. The same binary shows `note` only when `CLIER_AGENT` is present, so a user starting from `--help` cannot discover the logging flow.
findings.fail_note: Command surface changes by environment variable with no discoverable bridge from user mode.

steps.label: user-run-help
steps.stdout_excerpt:
Available Commands:
  attach
  list
  start
  stop
  tell
  view

steps.label: agent-run-help
steps.stdout_excerpt:
Available Commands:
  note        Record a progress note
  tell        Send a message to an agent

references.label: user-mode run help
references.excerpt: Use start to launch agents, tell to send them instructions, and attach to watch them work.

references.label: agent-mode run help
references.excerpt: Use `tell` to message another agent. Use `note` to record a work log entry.

findings.title: Documented hello-claude run lifecycle works end to end
findings.rationale: The built-in tutorial flow worked in practice: cloning `@clier/hello-claude`, starting a run, sending messages, observing them in `run view`, stopping the run, and removing the working copy all succeeded. `run attach` correctly refused inside an agent and told me to use a real terminal.

steps.label: tell
steps.command: unset CLIER_AGENT; clier run tell --run 20260418T143047-c01acd7f --to @clier/hello-claude "Have both team members greet each other and report the result."

findings.title: Working-copy edit, push, remote drift, and pull all reconcile visibly
findings.rationale: Editing the materialized `CLAUDE.md` in a cloned disposable team made `status` report one modified instruction, `push` created a new instruction version and cascaded both teams, server-side edits later surfaced as `behind` and `pin_outdated`, and `pull` brought the working copy back to the latest pinned graph.

findings.title: Dirty-workspace pull failure suggests the wrong next action
findings.rationale: The CLI correctly refused to pull over local modifications, but the returned hint was copied from remove-path logic rather than pull-path logic.
findings.stdout_excerpt: {"error":{"kind":"workspace_dirty","message":"working copy has uncommitted changes (jakeraft.qa-20260418232811-team/CLAUDE.md modified)","hint":"push or revert before removing"}}
findings.fail_note: The attempted operation was pull, but the hint tells the user what to do before removing.

findings.title: Successful create, fork, and delete responses are not immediately queryable
findings.rationale: Multiple mutations acknowledged success before the CLI could read them back. The pattern repeated across resource creation, fork, and deletion, so the issue is broader than one endpoint.
findings.fail_note: The CLI returns success without any hint that immediate reads may still fail or show stale data for about two seconds.

steps.label: child-then-root-create
steps.command: unset CLIER_AGENT; clier create team ...child && clier create team ...root
steps.stdout_excerpt: first child create returned version 1; immediate root create failed with {"error":{"kind":"resource_not_found","message":"resource jakeraft/qa-20260418232811-child does not exist"}}

steps.label: root-create-after-wait
steps.command: sleep 2; unset CLIER_AGENT; clier create team ...root

steps.label: fork-immediate-read
steps.command: unset CLIER_AGENT; clier fork @clier/greeting-prompt; clier get jakeraft/greeting-prompt
steps.stdout_excerpt: fork returned a new instruction object, but immediate get returned {"error":{"kind":"resource_not_found","message":"resource jakeraft/greeting-prompt does not exist"}}

steps.label: fork-read-after-wait
steps.command: sleep 2; unset CLIER_AGENT; clier get jakeraft/greeting-prompt

steps.label: delete-immediate-list
steps.command: unset CLIER_AGENT; clier delete ...; clier list jakeraft --query qa-20260418232811 --limit 20
steps.stdout_excerpt: delete returned {"deleted":"jakeraft/qa-20260418232811-team"} and peers, but immediate list still returned the same resources

steps.label: delete-read-after-wait
steps.command: sleep 2; unset CLIER_AGENT; clier list jakeraft --query qa-20260418232811 --limit 20

findings.title: Referenced skills can be deleted while teams still pin them
findings.rationale: A disposable root team and child team both pinned `qa-20260418232811-skill`, but `clier delete jakeraft/qa-20260418232811-skill` still returned success instead of blocking the destructive operation or requiring the refs to be removed first.
findings.fail_note: This allows broken or silently rewritten dependency graphs even though the team graph was still live on the server.

steps.label: team-before-delete
steps.stdout_excerpt: before deletion, the team pinned child v3 plus instruction v2 and settings; earlier status output showed both root and child tracking the skill

findings.title: Org subcommands require an undocumented @ prefix after creation
findings.rationale: `clier org create qa-20260418232811-org` succeeded, but the returned name was `@qa-20260418232811-org`. Follow-up commands with the help-text shape (`<org-name>`) were routed as user lookups until I retried with the `@`-prefixed form.
findings.fail_note: The create and members/invite/remove contracts disagree about whether org names are bare names or @-prefixed handles.

steps.label: create
steps.command: unset CLIER_AGENT; clier org create qa-20260418232811-org

steps.label: members-without-at
steps.command: unset CLIER_AGENT; clier org members qa-20260418232811-org

findings.title: Version-pinned fork syntax is parsed as part of the resource name
findings.rationale: `clier versions @clier/greeting-prompt` showed version 1 exists, but `clier fork @clier/greeting-prompt@1` failed by looking for a resource literally named `greeting-prompt@1` instead of either forking that version or explicitly rejecting the syntax.
findings.fail_note: The error shape hides whether version-pinned forks are unsupported or just misparsed.

findings.title: Primary outputs remained machine-parseable
findings.rationale: Across list/get/versions/config/open/run view, stdout stayed JSON with snake_case fields, empty collections were rendered as `[]` rather than `null`, and timestamps used RFC3339 forms (`Z` for server timestamps, local offset for runtime timestamps).

steps.label: empty-list
steps.command: unset CLIER_AGENT; clier list jakeraft --query qa-20260418232811-nope --limit 5

findings.title: Config get/set/view and dashboard open are usable
findings.rationale: Reading config values worked, setting `dashboard_url` and `workspace_dir` to their existing values succeeded without side effects, and `clier open dashboard` returned a machine-readable open result.

steps.label: config-set-dashboard-url
steps.command: unset CLIER_AGENT; clier config set dashboard-url http://localhost:5173

findings.title: Auth logout was not exercised inside the publish run
findings.rationale: The run needed a working authenticated session to create, delete, and finally push the report. `auth login` also immediately started a new GitHub device flow even while `auth status` already showed a valid login.
findings.skip_reason: Exercising `clier auth logout` would have intentionally broken the authenticated state required to finish the autonomous run, and recovery would have depended on manual device-flow approval.
